article_meta.title: San Francisco sues 10 major food manufacturers over ultra-processe

reasoning: The model repeatedly flagged quoted and clearly attributed statements as author bias and missed any defensible author-prose issues. Multiple attribution rule violations and false positives; no solid author-level bias identified.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: Flagged multiple direct quotes and attributed claims (Chiu quote; NAM quote; suit allegations) as author bias.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: All four items are not author voice; three are direct quotes and one is explicitly attributed to the lawsuit.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: Any potential author-level bias is minimal (e.g., headline phrasing about links without explicit attribution); missing it is a minor omission.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: Even aside from attribution errors, at least one label is clearly wrong (calling an attributed sentence 'Omission of Source Attribution'); others are only plausible if they were author voice.
